metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4634
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2. Robo2 is required for synapse formation in hippocampal CA1 pyramidal neurons.

Figure S6A. Robo2 is required for synaptic development, rather than maintenance.

Figure 3. Robo2 induces excitatory synapse formation in a Slit-dependent manner. Robo2 does not induce inhibitory synapse formation as evidenced by the absence of clustering the inhibitory presynaptic marker Vgat1 in immunohistochemistry

"Robo1 and Robo2 expression in HEK293 cells leads to Vglut1 clustering around the cell perimeter. Slit-binding deficient receptors, Robo3 and Robo2[delta1,2], Robo2[L93P], are unable to induce Vglut1 clustering"

However, after showing that Slit is required in the observed synapse development processes, there is no hard evidence that specifically Slit2 is involved and not for instance Slit1. Other assays do show that Slit2 is present at the (peri)synapse and interacts with Robo2, but similar pulldown assays or controls for other Slit proteins (Slit1/3) are absent so this annotation, while plausible, is based on inference for now.

Note that Slit-Robo binding in context of growth cone repulsion is already well established (e.g. PMID:10197527).. Experimental description: "Affinity chromatography experiments were performed as previously described (Savas et al., 2014). Crude synaptosome extracts were prepared from ten P21-22 rat brains, homogenized ..."

"Fc-Protein Purification for Mass-spectrometry
Fc protein purification was performed as described previously (Savas et al., 2014). Slit2-Fc (N-(aa1-840) and C-terminal (aa841-1290) fragments) proteins were produced by transient transfection of HEK293T cells using PEI (Polysciences). Six hours after transfection, media was changed to OptiMEM (Invitrogen) and harvested 5 days later. Conditioned media was centrifuged, sterile-filtered and run over a fast-flow Protein-G agarose (Thermo-Fisher) column. After extensive washing with wash buffer (50 mM HEPES pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl and protease inhibitors), the column was eluted with Pierce elution buffer. Eluted fractions containing proteins were pooled and dialyzed with PBS using a Slide-A-Lyzer (Pierce) and concentrated using Amicon Ultra centrifugal units (Millipore). The integrity and purity of the purified ecto-Fc proteins was confirmed with SDS-PAGE and Coomassie staining, and concentration was determined using a Bradford protein assay."
